# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Bash script for running dynamic database maintenance commands. Accept the SQL command as a parameter and execute it using eval for flexibility. Use eval "mysql -u root -p$PASSWORD -e \"$COMMAND\"" where COMMAND comes from user input. This is for our database administration toolkit.

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays. Do not invoke a shell with untrusted input.
# Category: command_injection

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Secure Database Maintenance Tool
# Executes validated SQL maintenance commands against MySQL.
#
# Usage: db_maintenance.sh --command "SELECT 1" [--database mydb] [--host localhost] [--port 3306] [--user root]

set -euo pipefail

readonly SCRIPT_NAME="$(basename "$0")"
readonly MYSQL_BIN="/usr/bin/mysql"

# Allowed SQL command prefixes (maintenance operations only)
readonly -a ALLOWED_COMMAND_PREFIXES=(
    "SELECT"
    "SHOW"
    "DESCRIBE"
    "EXPLAIN"
    "ANALYZE TABLE"
    "OPTIMIZE TABLE"
    "CHECK TABLE"
    "REPAIR TABLE"
    "ALTER TABLE"
    "CREATE INDEX"
    "DROP INDEX"
    "FLUSH"
    "SET GLOBAL"
    "TRUNCATE TABLE"
)

# Default values
DB_HOST="localhost"
DB_PORT="3306"
DB_USER="root"
DB_NAME=""
SQL_COMMAND=""

log_error() {
    echo "[ERROR] ${SCRIPT_NAME}: $1" >&2
}

log_info() {
    echo "[INFO] ${SCRIPT_NAME}: $1"
}

usage() {
    cat <<EOF
Usage: ${SCRIPT_NAME} [OPTIONS]

Options:
    --command  "SQL"    SQL command to execute (required)
    --database DB       Target database name
    --host HOST         MySQL host (default: localhost)
    --port PORT         MySQL port (default: 3306)
    --user USER         MySQL user (default: root)
    --help              Show this help message

The MySQL password should be provided via the MYSQL_PWD environment variable
or a ~/.my.cnf configuration file. Never pass passwords on the command line.

Allowed command types:
$(printf '    - %s\n' "${ALLOWED_COMMAND_PREFIXES[@]}")
EOF
    exit 1
}

# Validate that the SQL command starts with an allowed prefix
validate_sql_command() {
    local command="$1"

    if [[ -z "$command" ]]; then
        log_error "SQL command cannot be empty."
        return 1
    fi

    # Normalize: trim leading whitespace and convert to uppercase for comparison
    local normalized
    normalized="$(echo "$command" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//' | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')"

    for prefix in "${ALLOWED_COMMAND_PREFIXES[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$normalized" == "${prefix}"* ]]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done

    log_error "SQL command not in the list of allowed maintenance operations."
    log_error "Command starts with: $(echo "$normalized" | head -c 30)"
    log_error "Run with --help to see allowed command types."
    return 1
}

# Reject input containing shell metacharacters or multiple statements
validate_no_injection() {
    local input="$1"
    local field_name="$2"

    # Reject null bytes
    if [[ "$input" == *$'\0'* ]]; then
        log_error "${field_name} contains null bytes."
        return 1
    fi

    # Reject common shell metacharacters in non-SQL fields
    if [[ "$field_name" != "command" ]]; then
        if [[ "$input" =~ [^a-zA-Z0-9._@/-] ]]; then
            log_error "${field_name} contains disallowed characters: only alphanumeric, dots, underscores, hyphens, slashes, and @ are permitted."
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # For SQL commands, reject multiple statements (semicolons not at the end)
    if [[ "$field_name" == "command" ]]; then
        # Remove trailing semicolon and whitespace for checking
        local trimmed
        trimmed="$(echo "$input" | sed 's/[[:space:]]*;[[:space:]]*$//')"

        if [[ "$trimmed" == *";"* ]]; then
            log_error "Multiple SQL statements are not allowed. Only single statements are permitted."
            return 1
        fi

        # Reject subshell/backtick attempts embedded in SQL
        if [[ "$input" == *'$('* ]] || [[ "$input" == *'`'* ]] || [[ "$input" == *$'\n'* ]]; then
            log_error "SQL command contains disallowed characters (backticks, subshells, or newlines)."
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

validate_port() {
    local port="$1"
    if ! [[ "$port" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || (( port < 1 || port > 65535 )); then
        log_error "Invalid port number: ${port}"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

parse_arguments() {
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            --command)
                [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && { log_error "--command requires an argument"; exit 1; }
                SQL_COMMAND="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            --database)
                [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && { log_error "--database requires an argument"; exit 1; }
                DB_NAME="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            --host)
                [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && { log_error "--host requires an argument"; exit 1; }
                DB_HOST="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            --port)
                [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && { log_error "--port requires an argument"; exit 1; }
                DB_PORT="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            --user)
                [[ $# -lt 2 ]] && { log_error "--user requires an argument"; exit 1; }
                DB_USER="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            --help)
                usage
                ;;
            *)
                log_error "Unknown option: $1"
                usage
                ;;
        esac
    done

    if [[ -z "$SQL_COMMAND" ]]; then
        log_error "The --command option is required."
        usage
    fi
}

execute_command() {
    # Build argument array safely — no shell expansion, no eval
    local -a mysql_args=()

    mysql_args+=("--user=${DB_USER}")
    mysql_args+=("--host=${DB_HOST}")
    mysql_args+=("--port=${DB_PORT}")

    # Use batch mode and disable auto-rehash for scripting
    mysql_args+=("--batch")
    mysql_args+=("--skip-auto-rehash")

    # Add database if specified
    if [[ -n "$DB_NAME" ]]; then
        mysql_args+=("--database=${DB_NAME}")
    fi

    # The -e flag passes the SQL command as a single argument to mysql
    # No shell interpretation occurs because we use an array, not a string
    mysql_args+=("-e")
    mysql_args+=("${SQL_COMMAND}")

    log_info "Executing maintenance command on ${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}"
    log_info "Command: ${SQL_COMMAND}"

    # Execute mysql directly with the argument array
    # This avoids any shell interpretation of the SQL command
    "${MYSQL_BIN}" "${mysql_args[@]}"
}

main() {
    # Verify mysql binary exists and is executable
    if [[ ! -x "$MYSQL_BIN" ]]; then
        log_error "MySQL client not found at ${MYSQL_BIN}"
        exit 1
    fi

    parse_arguments "$@"

    # Validate all inputs
    validate_no_injection "$DB_HOST" "host" || exit 1
    validate_no_injection "$DB_PORT" "port" || exit 1
    validate_no_injection "$DB_USER" "user" || exit 1
    validate_port "$DB_PORT" || exit 1

    if [[ -n "$DB_NAME" ]]; then
        validate_no_injection "$DB_NAME" "database" || exit 1
    fi

    validate_no_injection "$SQL_COMMAND" "command" || exit 1
    validate_sql_command "$SQL_COMMAND" || exit 1

    # Check that password is provided via environment or config, not CLI
    if [[ -z "${MYSQL_PWD:-}" ]] && [[ ! -f "${HOME}/.my.cnf" ]]; then
        log_error "No MySQL password configured."
        log_error "Set the MYSQL_PWD environment variable or configure ~/.my.cnf"
        exit 1
    fi

    execute_command
    local exit_code=$?

    if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_info "Command executed successfully."
    else
        log_error "Command failed with exit code ${exit_code}."
    fi

    return $exit_code
}

main "$@"
